sections.title: At the temple before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You have news of Drezel for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where am I supposed to go again?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: The temple of Paterdomus where Drezel lives. It is but a short journey east from here. It lies south of the cliffs, at the source of the River Salve.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Don't worry, you can't miss it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a quest!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: A quest you say? Hmm, what an odd request to make of the king. It's funny you should mention it though, as there is something you can do for me.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Are you aware of Paterdomus? It's a temple east of here. It stands on the River Salve and guards the only passage into the deadly lands of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't think I know it...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Hmm, how strange that you don't. Well anyway, it has been some days since last I heard from Drezel, the priest who lives there.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Be a sport and go make sure that nothing untoward has happened to the silly old codger for me, would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. I don't have anything better to do right now.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Many thanks adventurer! I would have sent one of my squires but they wanted payment for it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you care about Drezel anyway?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well, that is a slightly impertinent question to ask of your king, but I shall overlook it this time.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: As you are no doubt aware, this kingdom worships Saradomin, the god of wisdom and order. As such, it is a peaceful place to live and prosper.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Paterdomus, the temple where Drezel lives, stands on the eastern border of Misthalin. It guards the only passage into the evil lands of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Evil?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Oh yes. Morytania is a fearful place, filled to the brim with accursed servants of the chaos god Zamorak. All of them are terrible, but none more so than the rulers of the region, the vampyres.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Thankfully, the sacred River Salve marks a natural border between Misthalin and Morytania. Together, the river and the temple prevent any invasion by the vampyres.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: The waters of the river are kept blessed with Saradomin's almighty power. This ensures our defences remain strong, as the vampyres cannot cross such a holy barrier.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Drezel is descended from one of the original Saradominist priests who first blessed the river. His job is to ensure nothing happens to the river that might allow the evil of Morytania to invade this land.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: This is the reason why the lack of communication from him bothers me somewhat, although I am sure nobody would dare to try and attack our kingdom!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I get a reward for this?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You will be rewarded in the knowledge that you have done the right thing and assisted the king of Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Soooooo...... that would be a 'no' then?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: That is correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Drezel?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Drezel is the priest who lives at Paterdomus, the temple to the east. You're supposed to be making sure that nothing bad has happened to him. Remember?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooooooooh, THAT Drezel. Yup, I remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. That sounds boring.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, I dare say it does. I wouldn't even have mentioned it had you not seemed to be looking for something to do anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

sections.title: Knocking on the door after accepting to kill the dog

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: SHHHH! It's the adventurer!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Ah! I want you to go kill the horrible dog in the mausoleum for me! You can use the entrance out there.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: You'll do this for good old Delzig, won't ya buddy?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Drezel!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: *cough* for good old Drezel, right buddy?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: Excuse me...oh, wait, I thought you were someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. Have a good day!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: And yourself.

sections.title: Atempting to open the gate without the key to unlock it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... From the looks of things, it seems as though somebody has been trying to force this gate open. It's still securely locked however.

sections.title: Talking to King Roald after accepting to kill the dog

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You have news of Drezel for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I went to the temple like you asked me to... and I spoke to someone inside...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Ah, well that must have been Drezel then. What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... he seemed to be having some kind of trouble. He asked for my help.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well I expect you to offer him your full assistance in whatever he needs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You will have to excuse me then. I am very busy as I have a kingdom to run!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Who are you and what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ummmm.....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Roald sent me to check on Drezel.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Psst... Hey... Who's Roald? Who's Drezel?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: Uh... Isn't Drezel that dude upstairs? Oh, wait, Roald's the king of Misthalin right?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: He is? Aww man...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Hey, you deal with this okay.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: He's just coming! Wait a second!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Hello, my name is Drevil.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Drezel!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: I mean Drezel. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, as I say, the king sent me to make sure everything's okay with you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: And, uh, what would you do if everything wasn't okay with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure. Ask you what help you need I suppose.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Ah, good, well, I don't think...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: Psst... Hey... The dog!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: OH! Yes, of course!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Will you do me a favour, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. I'm a helpful person!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: HAHAHAHA! Really? Thanks buddy! You see that mausoleum out there? There's a horrible big dog underneath it that I'd like you to kill for me! It's been really bugging me! Barking all the time and stuff!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Please kill it for me buddy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okey-dokey, one dead dog coming up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope. Something about all this is very suspicious...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Get lost then! I have important things to do, as sure as my name is Dibzil.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Drezel!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Drezel. Now, go away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I just moved in next door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I borrow a cup of coffee?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: What next door? What's coffee? Who ARE you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear this place is of historic interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I come in and have a wander around? Possibly look at some antiques or buy something from your gift shop?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Pssst... Hey... Is this place of historic interest?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: I dunno. I guess it might be. Does it matter?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: I suppose not.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Clear off! You can't come in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The council sent me to check your pipes.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: They did? Ummm....

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Psst... Are there any pipes in here, you reckon?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: I dunno... I don't think so...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: We don't have any thanks! Bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing. Never mind.

sections.title: Letting the monks know you did the deed

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: You again? What do you want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed that dog for you.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Really? Hey, that's great!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Yeah thanks a lot buddy!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: HAHAHAHAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so funny?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: Nothing buddy! We're just so grateful!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: Yeah, maybe you should go tell the king what a great job you did buddy!

sections.title: Talking to King Roald about the now dead dog

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You have news of Drezel for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I spoke to the guys at the temple. They said they were being bothered by a dog in the mausoleum, so I went and killed it for them. No problem.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: YOU DID WHAT???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Are you a complete imbecile?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: That mausoleum contains the only passage between Morytania and Misthalin! Not only that, it's built right over the source of the River Salve!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did I make a mistake?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: YES YOU DID!!!!! Without that 'dog', there's nothing to stop someone from entering the mausoleum to sabotage the blessings on the river. Thanks to you, all of Misthalin is now at risk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: B-but... Drezel told me to...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: No, you absolute cretin! Obviously some fiend has done something to Drezel and tricked your feeble intellect into helping them kill that guard dog!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Now you get back there and do whatever is necessary to safeguard the kingdom from attack, or I will see you beheaded for high treason!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Y-yes your highness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Why haven't you ensured the border with Morytania is secure yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay... I'm going, I'm going... There's no need to shout...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: NO NEED TO SHOUT???

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Listen, and listen well, and see if your puny mind can comprehend this: if the border is not protected, then we are all at the mercy of the vampyres!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I would say that me shouting at you for your incompetence is the LEAST of your worries right now. NOW GO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There we go, one dead dog. I should go and tell Drezel.

sections.title: Attempting to open the coffin on the top floor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like there's something alive inside it. I don't think it would be a very good idea to open it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Oh! You do not appear to be one of those Zamorakians who imprisoned me! Who are you and why are you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My name's [player name]. King Roald sent me to find out what was going on at the temple. I take it you are Drezel?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: That's right! Oh, praise be to Saradomin! All is not yet lost!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I feared that when those Zamorakians attacked this place and imprisoned me up here, Misthalin would be doomed!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: If they should manage to desecrate the holy River Salve, we will be defenceless against the vampyres of Morytania!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is the river such a good defence?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I'm not sure if this is the best time for a history lesson. I suppose I can give you a brief overview though. It might help you understand our next steps.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It is said that many years ago, the land that is now Morytania was a peaceful Saradominist kingdom. Alas, all that ended when the vampyres invaded, claiming the land as their own.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Morytania wasn't enough for them though. Soon, they set their sights on these lands as well.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Misthalin would have quickly fallen to the vampyres, were it not for the bravery of the Seven Priestly Warriors.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: They led the armies of Misthalin against the vampyres in a great battle that took place right where this temple stands.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: For ten days and nights they fought, never sleeping, never eating, fuelled by their desire to make the world a better place for humans to live.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: On the eleventh day they were to be joined by reinforcements. However, when those reinforcements arrived, they found the Seven Priestly Warriors and their forces had been slain by the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Their sacrifice was not in vain though. Before they were defeated, the Seven Priestly Warriors managed to bless the Salve with the holy power of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Their blessings remain to this day, ensuring no vampyre can cross the river.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The temple here was built soon after. It guards the source of the River Salve, and acts as the only passage between Misthalin and Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Once the temple was complete, the bodies of the Seven Priestly Warriors were laid to rest in tombs of honour in the mausoleum below. On the top of each tomb, a golden gift was placed as a mark of respect.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: A great statue was also built over the river, so that all who might try and cross into Misthalin would know that these lands are protected by Saradomin himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I can see how the river protects the border. Where do these Zamorakians fit in though?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, as much as it saddens me to say so adventurer, Lord Saradomin's presence has not been felt on the land for many years now.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Of course, while we know that he still watches over us, his power here is not as strong as it once was.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I fear this group of Zamorakians intends to take advantage of that. If they can somehow pollute the Salve, the blessings on the river might fail.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: If that happens, there won't be anything to stop an invasion from Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: So what do you say adventurer? Will you aid me and all of Misthalin in foiling this Zamorakian plot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Thank you, adventurer. There is a well in the mausoleum beneath the temple that provides direct access to the source of the Salve. That will be where the Zamorakians will attempt their sabotage.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: We need to get to that well. However, the immediate problem is that I'm trapped in this cell.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The key must be somewhere nearby. One of the Zamorakians might have it, or they might have hidden it somewhere. That's not the only problem though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What else is there?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: When the Salve was blessed, a small number of vampyres were left trapped on this side of it. They were heavily weakened, but still posed a dangerous threat.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: This coffin here contains one of those vampyres. The Zamorakians somehow found it and brought it here with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. It doesn't seem to be posing a threat right now though.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: No, but as those fiendish Zamorakians pointed out to me with delight, as I am the descendant of one of the Seven Priestly Warriors who blessed the river, it will likely recognise the smell of my blood.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: If I get too close, it will probably wake up and kill me, very slowly and painfully.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could kill it somehow?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: No adventurer, you should not risk waking it. Even now, it could pose a huge threat.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The priests of old would incapacitate trapped vampyres with the blessings of Saradomin. I suggest you do the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. So, I need to find the key to your cell and do something about the vampyre. Got it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Oooooh... I knew it was too good to be true... Then leave me to my fate villain, there's no need to taunt me as well as keeping me imprisoned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what now?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, let's just say if we cannot undo whatever damage has been done here, the entire land is in grave peril!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: So what do you say adventurer? Will you aid me and all of Misthalin in foiling this Zamorakian plot?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: How goes it adventurer? Any luck in finding the key to the cell or a way of stopping the vampyre yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet...

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well don't give up adventurer! That key must be around here somewhere! I know none of those Zamorakians ever got very far from this building!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know that?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I could hear them laughing about some gullible fool that they tricked into killing the guard dog at the monument.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Honestly, what kind of idiot would go around killing things just because a stranger told them to? What kind of oafish, numb-skulled, dim-witted...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I get the picture!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about this temple?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: It is said that many years ago, the land that is now Morytania was a peaceful Saradominist kingdom. Alas, all that ended when the vampyres invaded, claiming the land as their own.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Morytania wasn't enough for them though. Soon, they set their sights on these lands as well.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Misthalin would have quickly fallen to the vampyres, were it not for the bravery of the Seven Priestly Warriors.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: They led the armies of Misthalin against the vampyres in a great battle that took place right where this temple stands.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: For ten days and nights they fought, never sleeping, never eating, fuelled by their desire to make the world a better place for humans to live.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: On the eleventh day they were to be joined by reinforcements. However, when those reinforcements arrived, they found the Seven Priestly Warriors and their forces had been slain by the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Their sacrifice was not in vain though. Before they were defeated, the Seven Priestly Warriors managed to bless the Salve with the holy power of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Their blessings remain to this day, ensuring no vampyre can cross the river.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The temple here was built soon after. It guards the source of the River Salve, and acts as the only passage between Misthalin and Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Once the temple was complete, the bodies of the Seven Priestly Warriors were laid to rest in tombs of honour in the mausoleum below. On the top of each tomb, a golden gift was placed as a mark of respect.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: A great statue was also built over the river, so that all who might try and cross into Misthalin would know that these lands are protected by Saradomin himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the information. I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You have news of Drezel for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do indeed, sire. He has been imprisoned by some Zamorakian monks.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: What? This is wholly unacceptable! I order you to do all that you can to free Drezel immediately!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was doing that anyway.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Ah, I see. In that case keep up the good work.

sections.title: Talking to Drezel with the golden key

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: How goes it adventurer? Any luck in finding the key to the cell or a way of stopping the vampyre yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this key I took from one of those Zamorakian monks!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work adventurer! Quickly, try it on the door, and see if it will free me!

sections.title: Exchanging the golden key with the iron key
Talking to Drezel with the iron key

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: How goes it adventurer? Any luck in finding the key to the cell or a way of stopping the vampyre yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have this key I took from one of the monuments underground.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work adventurer! Quickly, try it on the door, and see if it will free me!

sections.title: Using the iron key on the door

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The key fit the lock! You're free to leave now!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer! Unfortunately, as you know, I cannot risk waking the vampyre in that coffin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any ideas about dealing with the vampyre?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, the water of the Salve should still have enough power to work against the vampyre, even if the Zamorakians have desecrated it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Maybe you should try and get hold of some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Have you freed Drezel yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I found the key to his cell and unlocked it, but there's a vampyre in there stopping him leaving.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: A vampyre? Well, I order you to do something about it at once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I was planning on doing that anyway.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Good work! Always a place for quick thinkers in my kingdom!

sections.title: Attempting to pour normal water on the coffin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think pouring normal water on the coffin is going to help...

sections.title: Attempting to pour murky water on the coffin

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This water doesn't look particularly holy to me... I think I'd better check with Drezel first.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Oh! Thank you! You have found the key!

sections.title: Talking to Drezel with blessed water

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The key fit the lock! You're free to leave now!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer! Unfortunately, as you know, I cannot risk waking the vampyre in that coffin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some blessed water from the Salve in this bucket. Do you think it will help against that vampyre?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Yes! Great idea! If his coffin is doused in the blessed water he will be unable to leave it! Use it on his coffin, quickly!

sections.title: Pouring blessed water on the coffin
Attempting to open the coffin again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The vampyre should be dealt with. I'd still best not risk it though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Have you freed Drezel yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sire.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Aha! Good work adventurer! What news of the border defences against Morytania?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, uh, don't know about that...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well get back and find out at once! This is a matter of national security and if I find out we are vulnerable I will hold you personally responsible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This water doesn't look particularly holy to me... I think I'd better check with Drezel first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The key fit the lock! You're free to leave now!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer! Unfortunately, as you know, I cannot risk waking the vampyre in that coffin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some water from the Salve. It seems to have been desecrated though. Do you think you could bless it for me?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Yes, good thinking adventurer! Give it to me, I will bless it!

sections.title: After Drezel is freed and the Vampyre is sealed

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I just need to ensure this vampyre is trapped. I will meet you down in the mausoleum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Yes, citizen. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that job I'm doing...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Have you ensured Misthalin's border is fully secured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'm working on it though.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Good, good.

sections.title: Attempting to pass the holy barrier before securing the barrier

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: STOP!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I go through there?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: No, you cannot! It is taking all of my willpower to hold that barrier in place. You must restore the sanctity of the Salve as soon as possible!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I poured the blessed water over the coffin. I think that should trap the vampyre in there long enough for you to escape.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent work adventurer! I am free at last! Let me ensure that evil vampyre is trapped for good. I will then meet you down in the mausoleum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Bring me fifty rune essence so that I can undo the damage done by those Zamorakians.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. Glad you made it. Things are worse than I feared down here. I'm not sure if I will be able to repair the damage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what's happened?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Sadly, with the guard dog gone, there was nothing to stop those Zamorakians from entering the mausoleum. From what I can tell, they have used some kind of evil potion to pollute the Salve.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The well here is built right over the source of the river. That means that their potion will spread all the way down the Salve, disrupting the blessings placed upon it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Before long, there will be nothing to stop the vampyres from invading Misthalin at their leisure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can we do to prevent that?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: This passage is currently the only route between Morytania and Misthalin. The barrier here draws power from the river.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I have managed to reinforce the barrier, but I must continue focussing on it to keep it intact. Although the passage is safe for now, I do not know how long I can keep it that way.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: This passage could be the least of our worries soon though. Before long, the vampyres will be able to cross at any point of the river. We won't be able to reinforce all of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Couldn't you bless the river to purify it? Like you did with the water I took from the well?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: No, that would not work. The power I have from Saradomin is not great enough to cleanse an entire river of this foul Zamorakian pollutant.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: However, I do have one other idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I believe we might be able to soak up the evil magic that the potion has released into the river.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I'm sure you know of runestones, used by mages to power their spells. The essence used to create these stones absorbs magical potential from runic altars to be released later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think we could use some rune essence to absorb the magic they've released into the Salve?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Exactly. If you could bring me fifty essence, I should be able to reverse the damage done. Be quick though. The longer we wait, the worse things will get.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much more essence do I need to bring you?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I need [1-50] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I brought you some rune essence.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Quickly, give them to me!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Thank you. I need [1-50] more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I brought you some rune essence.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You have brought me notes saying 'I promise to pay the bearer on demand the listed number of Rune Essence.' How is that supposed to help me?

sections.title: Giving Drezel the last of the essence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I brought you some more essence.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Quickly, give them to me!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Excellent! That should do it! I will bless these stones and place them within the well. With the river safe, Misthalin should be protected from the vampyres once more!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Please take this dagger. It has been handed down within my family for generations and is filled with the power of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You will find that it has the ability to prevent werewolves from adopting their wolf form in combat. Hopefully it comes in useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I pass through that barrier now?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Into Morytania? Yes, you can. The barrier will prevent the servants of Zamorak from entering Misthalin, but it is safe for those blessed by Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I must warn you though, Morytania is an evil land. The region is filled with creatures and monsters more terrifying than any you'll find on this side of the Salve.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Worst of all are the vampyres, the rulers of Morytania. Be very cautious of their kind. Many are completely immune to human weaponry. Those that aren't, are still very hard to kill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful. Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The first settlement you'll likely come across is Canifis, a town of werewolves. They probably won't attack you unless provoked, but you should still be cautious.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You should keep your Wolfbane dagger to hand. It will prevent them from taking on their wolf form.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: One last thing. While in Morytania, stay alert for any mention of the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Myreque?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Yes. They're a band of freedom fighters. They fight to protect the humans trapped in Morytania from the tyranny of the vampyres. Their hope is to one day rid the region of all evil.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Veliaf, the leader of their Mort Myre group, is an old friend. I imagine they'd appreciate the help of someone like you, if you can find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep an eye out for them.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Right, I think that's everything. Just the blessing itself now. Hold still a moment.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Good luck out there. Stay safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you. See you later.
